reasoning: Thomas Ceccon's 100m backstroke WR of 51.60 was set in 2022. The most recent major meet (2025 World Championships in July/August) saw a winning time of 51.85 by Pieter Coetze, indicating no one is immediately threatening the 51.60 mark in long course. Swimming world records typically last years and are usually broken during major taper meets (World Championships, Olympics, or peak National Trials). January and February generally lack major long course taper meets, making a WR highly unlikely for the first two resolution dates. By early April, a few countries (like Australia or Japan) may host national trials, posing a slightly higher but still very low risk to the record. Therefore, the probabilities remain very high but decrease slightly as the time horizon extends to April.
